name: Wrigley Field
transit: Red Line — Addison stop is 1 block from the main gate. Fastest option on sellout nights.
parking_tips: Wrigleyville has over 20 private surface lots within 4 blocks. Book at least 48 hours out for weekend games — spots sell out fast. Lots on Seminary Ave and Racine Ave tend to be cheaper than those directly on Clark St. Street parking on residential streets east of the park is free after 6pm but fills up 2 hours before first pitch.
best_lots: Seminary Ave corridor offers the best price-to-distance ratio. Clark St lots are closest but charge a premium.
worst_lots: Avoid lots on Addison west of the park — long walk and heavy post-game traffic.
arrive_recommendation: 90 minutes before first pitch for weekend games, 60 minutes for weekday games

faq.q: How early should I arrive for parking at Wrigley Field?
faq.a: For weekend games and big matchups, arrive at least 90 minutes before first pitch. Private lots in Wrigleyville fill up fast. For weekday games you can usually find a spot 60 minutes out.

faq.q: What is the cheapest parking near Wrigley Field?
faq.a: Surface lots on Seminary Ave and Racine Ave typically run $20-30 for day games, which is cheaper than the premium lots directly on Clark St. Booking online in advance almost always beats walk-up prices.

faq.q: Is there free parking near Wrigley Field?
faq.a: Some residential streets east of the park (Kenmore, Winthrop) have free street parking after 6pm. These spots fill up 2+ hours before evening games. For day games, paid lots are your best option.

faq.q: Can I take the train to Wrigley Field?
faq.a: Yes — the Red Line Addison stop is one block from the main gate. It's the fastest way in and out on sellout nights and avoids post-game traffic entirely.

faq.q: How much does parking cost at Wrigley Field?
faq.a: Private lots typically range from $20-45 depending on the game and how far in advance you book. World Series and playoff games can go significantly higher. Booking online in advance saves 20-40% versus walk-up.

meta_title: Wrigley Field Parking — Best Lots, Prices & Tips | GetToTheGate
meta_description: Find and reserve parking near Wrigley Field. Compare prices on nearby lots, get insider tips on the best spots, and book before you leave the house.

name: United Center
transit: CTA Pink/Green Line — Medical/Halsted stop is 0.5 miles. Free shuttle runs on event nights.
parking_tips: The United Center has its own official lots but they're expensive. Private lots on Madison St and Warren Blvd are cheaper and only slightly further. The area west of the arena has lower prices than the lots directly adjacent. Book online to lock in rates — walk-up prices surge on big game nights.
best_lots: Warren Blvd corridor and Damen Ave lots offer best value. United Center official Lot E is closest but priciest.
worst_lots: Avoid unattended lots on side streets without security on big game nights.
arrive_recommendation: 60-75 minutes before tipoff or puck drop

faq.q: What is the best parking for United Center events?
faq.a: Private lots on Warren Blvd and Madison St west of the arena offer the best combination of price and convenience. Official United Center lots are closest but charge a premium.

faq.q: How much does United Center parking cost?
faq.a: Official lots run $30-50 per event. Private lots nearby typically range from $15-35. Booking online in advance saves significantly versus walk-up prices.

faq.q: Is there public transit to the United Center?
faq.a: The CTA Pink and Green lines stop at Medical/Halsted, about 0.5 miles away. A free shuttle runs between the stop and the arena on event nights.

faq.q: How early should I arrive for United Center parking?
faq.a: 60-75 minutes before tipoff gets you a good spot without too much waiting. For playoff games and major concerts, arrive 90 minutes early.

meta_title: United Center Parking — Best Lots, Prices & Tips | GetToTheGate
meta_description: Find and reserve parking near the United Center in Chicago. Bulls and Blackhawks games, concerts and more — book your spot before you leave the house.

name: Fenway Park
transit: Green Line B/C/D — Kenmore stop is 5 minutes walk. Best option for Red Sox games.
parking_tips: Fenway is surrounded by private garages and lots in the Fenway/Kenmore neighborhood. The area has some of the best pre-booked parking inventory in any MLB market. Garages on Boylston St and Brookline Ave book up fast for Saturday games. The Landmark Center garage offers a good balance of price and walking distance.
best_lots: Landmark Center garage and Boylston St garages offer best value within walking distance.
worst_lots: Surface lots east of the park charge premium prices for a longer walk.
arrive_recommendation: 75 minutes before first pitch for weekend games

faq.q: Where is the best parking near Fenway Park?
faq.a: The Landmark Center garage and private lots along Boylston St offer the best combination of price and walking distance. Book online at least 24 hours ahead for weekend games.

faq.q: How much does parking cost near Fenway Park?
faq.a: Expect $25-50 for most events. Garages along Boylston St and Brookline Ave typically run $30-45. Pre-booking saves 20-30% versus walk-up rates.

faq.q: Can I take the subway to Fenway Park?
faq.a: Yes — the Green Line Kenmore stop is a 5-minute walk to the main entrance. It's by far the easiest way to get to Fenway for Red Sox games.

faq.q: Is parking hard to find near Fenway Park?
faq.a: Weekend games and big matchups sell out parking fast. Book online 24-48 hours in advance. Weekday games have more availability but prices still surge at walk-up.

name: Madison Square Garden
transit: Penn Station is directly below MSG. A/C/E and 1/2/3 subway lines. Best city to skip driving entirely.
parking_tips: MSG sits directly above Penn Station — transit is genuinely the best option here. If you must drive, dozens of private garages within 3 blocks. The blocks west toward 10th Ave have cheaper rates than those directly on 7th or 8th Ave. Book way in advance for Knicks playoffs, Rangers playoffs, or major concerts.
best_lots: Garages on 9th and 10th Ave offer best value — slightly further walk but significantly cheaper.
worst_lots: Garages directly on 7th Ave and 8th Ave charge Manhattan premium prices.
arrive_recommendation: 60 minutes before — or just take the subway

faq.q: Should I drive to Madison Square Garden?
faq.a: Honestly, no — Penn Station is directly below MSG and multiple subway lines stop there. Transit is faster, cheaper, and less stressful than driving in Midtown Manhattan.

faq.q: How much does parking cost near Madison Square Garden?
faq.a: Manhattan parking near MSG runs $30-70+ depending on event and time. Garages on 9th and 10th Ave are cheaper than those on 7th and 8th Ave.

faq.q: What is the closest parking garage to MSG?
faq.a: There are dozens of garages within 2-3 blocks. The Icon Parking garages on 8th Ave are among the closest. Book online — walk-up rates at Manhattan garages are significantly higher.

name: Dodger Stadium
address: 1000 Vin Scully Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90012
transit: Limited transit. Dodger Stadium Express runs from Union Station on game days — $8 round trip.
parking_tips: Dodger Stadium has massive on-site parking but private lots nearby are significantly cheaper. The stadium lots cost $35 walk-up — private lots in the surrounding area run $15-25. The Dodger Stadium Express from Union Station is a great option if you want to avoid driving altogether.
best_lots: Private lots on Academy Rd and Stadium Way offer 30-50% savings over official stadium parking.
worst_lots: Official stadium preferred lots — expensive and no better than general parking for most games.
arrive_recommendation: 75 minutes before first pitch — LA traffic is unpredictable

faq.q: How much is parking at Dodger Stadium?
faq.a: Official stadium lots charge $35 walk-up. Private lots in the surrounding area run $15-25 and are only a short walk further. Book online to lock in the cheaper rate.

faq.q: Is there cheap parking near Dodger Stadium?
faq.a: Yes — private lots on Academy Rd and along Stadium Way are 30-50% cheaper than official stadium parking. Book online in advance for the best rates.

faq.q: Can I take public transit to Dodger Stadium?
faq.a: The Dodger Stadium Express shuttle runs from Union Station on game days for $8 round trip. It's the most stress-free way to get to the stadium without dealing with LA traffic.

faq.q: How early should I leave for Dodger Stadium?
faq.a: LA traffic is notoriously unpredictable. Give yourself 75-90 minutes from most parts of the city. If you're coming from the west side during rush hour, leave even earlier.

parking_tips: Downtown LA has hundreds of private garages and lots within walking distance. The Figueroa Corridor between the arena and the Convention Center has the densest parking inventory in the area. Pre-booking is essential for Lakers and Kings playoff games — lots sell out days in advance.
best_lots: Garages on Figueroa St and Flower St offer best proximity. Surface lots on Pico Blvd are cheaper.
worst_lots: Lots immediately adjacent to the arena charge event premiums — venture one block further for significant savings.
